Clearances & EIA

Secure your license to operate with scientific precision. We manage the entire Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process and secure critical CTE, CTO, and EC clearances for industrial projects.

Get Clearance

What's Included

Regulatory pathway for high-impact industrial projects.

CTE / CTO & Environmental Clearance (EC)
Comprehensive Impact Assessment (EIA)
Baseline Ecological & Social Monitoring
Public Consultation & Hearing Strategy

Regulatory roadblocks

Delays in clearances can cost millions in project overhead.

01

Technical Gaps

Incomplete baseline data or flawed air/water modeling in EIA reports leads to immediate rejection by expert appraisal committees.

02

Procedural Delays

The SPCB and MoEFCC clearance cycles are complex. Mismanaging the submission timeline can cause months of unnecessary downtime.

03

Post-EC Compliance

Getting the clearance is only half the battle. Maintaining the mandatory six-monthly compliance reports is critical to avoid legal action.

The Clearance Protocol

A technical path from project planning to operation.

01

Scoping

  • Category Screening
  • Baseline Monitoring
  • Public Consultation
02

Modeling

  • Air Dispersion
  • Water Balance
  • Noise Mapping
03

Liaison

  • SPCB Submissions
  • Technical Defense
  • Public Hearings
04

Clearance

  • EC Procurement
  • CTE/CTO Grants
  • Compliance Audit

Regulatory Assets

Full documentation suite for legal compliance.

EIA Reports
EMP Strategies
6-Monthly Compliance
Audit Ready CTO

Regulatory Frameworks

Navigation through MoEFCC and SPCB clearance protocols.

EIA Categorization
  • Category A MoEFCC (Central) Clearance
  • Category B1 SEIAA (State) with Public Hearing
  • Category B2 SEIAA (State) exempt from EIA/PH
  • EIA Notification 2006 (and subsequent amendments)
Standard Clearances
  • CTE / CTO Water & Air Act Consents (SPCB)
  • CGWA NOC Groundwater abstraction permits
  • Coastal CRZ Clearance for marine/coastal projects
  • Post-EC Compliance Six-monthly monitoring reports

Clearance FAQs

Expert guidance on regulatory approval pathways.

The timeline for a Category A or B1 project typically ranges from 8 to 14 months. This includes baseline data collection (one full season, excluding monsoon), public hearing, preparation of the final EIA report, and technical appraisal by the Expert Appraisal Committee (EAC) or SEAC.
Per the 2006 Notification, public consultation is mandatory for all projects in Category A and Category B1, unless explicitly exempted (e.g., projects in designated industrial estates or small-scale expansions with minimal impact). We manage the technical documentation and liaison for public hearings.
Consent to Establish (CTE) is required *before* starting actual construction or setting up machinery. Consent to Operate (CTO) is applied for *after* the plant is constructed but *before* trial production begins. Operating without a valid CTO is a severe legal violation.
The Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) requires a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for extracting groundwater for industrial and commercial use. This requires a technical hydrogeological report and a commitment to rainwater harvesting / recharge projects to offset usage.
NABET Accredited MoEFCC Liaison SPCB Specialist EIA Approved
